descriptions.text:
スーパー競輪は、プレイヤーが競輪イベントの競技者としての夢を実現できるシミュレーションゲームです。主人公の名前、彼女、ライバルを選択した後、プレイヤーはアパートからスタートし、日本で最高の競輪レーサーになるための道を歩み始めます。ゲームには軽いRPG要素があり、プレイヤーは資金を使って回復アイテムを購入したり、衣装を変更したりできます。また、参加していないレースに賭けて追加の現金を得ることも可能です。

さらに、ゲームには2人から4人で楽しめるパーティーモードがあり、これはメインゲームのギャンブル要素を単純化したもので、各プレイヤーが賭けを通じて最大の配当を得ることを競います。

Super Keirin is a Japan-exclusive video game that allows players to become cyclists.

Cute anime-style graphics are employed throughout the game; stating that the premise of the game is less serious than other Super NES cycling video games.

The game plays like a miniature version of a role-playing video game.

Before each race, players has the ability to buy supplies from a drug store, a 24-hour variety store, and a clothing store for a certain amount of yen per purchase. Interaction with non-player characters is possible while at the shopping district. Players can also gamble by placing wagers on other bicycle racing events,[5] buying parimutuel tickets with the chance of winning a respectable sum. Acquiring a newspaper to pick up results from other races can be done within the downtown portion of the game.

There are nine competitors and five laps in a typical race. Every track that can be raced on using a bicycle is an velodrome with four superelevated turns that are typical in most velodromes. Riders are not always traveling at full speed or at a specific radius; making a balance between aggressive riding and passive riding a must. All information for particular races are viewable on an information bank that is included with the racing action. Players must choose between story and party modes that provide a different perspective of the bicycle racing lifestyle.
